hooks.prompt: The stop-check script above reports your session state. If SAVE_NEEDED=true, you MUST run session_checkpoint NOW with: (1) summary of what was done (files, commits, DB changes, decisions), (2) next_steps with specific pending items. NO EXCEPTIONS. If SAVE_NEEDED=false AND your response was purely answering a question with zero tool calls, you may skip. Otherwise SAVE.

CRITICAL: Context compaction is about to happen. The auto-save script just ran but it only saves a minimal checkpoint. You MUST NOW save DETAILED progress to dev_tasks via execute_sql with: (1) EVERY file changed, (2) EVERY commit hash, (3) EVERY CRM/DB update made, (4) exact state of in-progress work, (5) what is PENDING with specific details. Also sysdoc_update('session-context') if system state changed. This is your LAST CHANCE — after compaction you will NOT remember any of this.

Session starting. MANDATORY FIRST STEP: Read the git pull result above — if it pulled new commits, acknowledge what changed. Then follow CLAUDE.md Session Start protocol: (1) sysdoc_read('session-context'), (2) query dev_tasks for BOTH pending work (status in_progress/todo) AND recently completed (status done, ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 3) so you know what was JUST done, (3) git status + recent commits. Present: 'Ultimo lavoro completato' + 'In sospeso' + 'Prossimi passi' (from progress_log PENDING of most recent task). Then ask 'Su cosa lavoriamo?'
